steps.title: Undo the square root
steps.content: Start with $$r=\sqrt{5w-3}$$ Square both sides to remove the radical: $$r^2=5w-3$$

steps.title: Solve for w
steps.content: Add $3$ to both sides, then divide by $5$: $$r^2+3=5w$$ $$w=\frac{r^2+3}{5}=\frac{r^2}{5}+\frac{3}{5}$$

steps.title: Use the condition on w
steps.content: The condition $w\gt \frac{3}{5}$ makes $5w-3$ positive, so squaring both sides is valid for the original square-root equation.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: The expression $w=\frac{r^2}{5}+\frac{3}{5}$ matches the correct answer, C.
